funding_references.funder_name: Swiss National Centres of Competence in Research (NCCR) Robotics

title: BNCI 2022-001 EEG Correlates of Difficulty Level dataset
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 13 healthy subjects performing a visuomotor learning task involving simulated drone piloting through waypoints of varying difficulty levels. The study investigates real-time decoding of subjective difficulty from EEG signals to enable adaptive closed-loop learning, comparing algorithmic difficulty adjustment with subject-controlled progression. Data include offline and online sessions with preprocessed 25-channel central EEG recordings (from original 64-channel acquisition) sampled at 256 Hz, alongside behavioral markers of task performance.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Biosemi ActiveTwo system with 64 channels at 256 Hz sampling rate (downsampled from 2048 Hz). Preprocessing included bandpass filtering (1-40 Hz, Butterworth order 14), SPHARA spatial filtering, common-average re-referencing, ICA-based EOG artifact removal, and amplitude thresholding (>50 µV rejection). Subjects performed a visuomotor task controlling simulated drone roll and pitch via joystick while navigating circular waypoints. Offline sessions consisted of 32 trajectories with v-shaped difficulty design; online sessions included 12 trajectories per condition with adaptive difficulty levels. Classification used LDA and elastic net regularization on power spectral density features.
